name: Presa de Manzanares el Real – circular from Colmenar via Vías Verdes – gravel
name_original: Presa de Manzanares el Real – circular desde Colmenar por Vías Verdes – gravel
region_intro: Touring cycling around Colmenar Viejo is characterized by its location at the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region includes parts of the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, providing protected natural settings. Landscapes feature extensive grasslands, areas shaped by traditional granite mining, and views of the mountainous backdrop. This diverse environment supports a network of routes suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Colmenar Viejo The most popular touring cycling route is Presa de Manzanares el Real – circular from Colmenar via Vías Verdes – gravel, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route features gravel sections and passes through Vías Verdes near Manzanares el Real. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Covacho Waterfall – Batán Bridge loop from Colmenar Viejo, a difficult 37.5 miles (60.4 km) path. This route includes natural features like the Covacho Waterfall and the historic Batán Bridge. Local touring cyclists also love the Santillana Reservoir – Manzanares el Real Viewpoint loop from Colmenar Viejo, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail leading through areas around the Santillana Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Colmenar Viejo is defined by its Sierra de Guadarrama foothills, the Manzanares Upper Basin Regional Park, and granite-influenced landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Colmenar Viejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 1,800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Colmenar Viejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Colmenar Viejo offers a wide network of touring cycling routes. Komoot alone features 78 routes, with options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Yes, there are several easy touring cycling routes in Colmenar Viejo. Komoot lists 3 easy routes, perfect for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride through the scenic landscapes.

Q: What are some of the natural attractions I can see while cycling in Colmenar Viejo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through parts of the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, enjoy views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, or explore areas like the Dehesa de Navalvillar. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10430811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall – Batán Bridge loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, takes you past the Covacho Waterfall and the historic Batán Bridge.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Colmenar Viejo?
A: Yes, many routes in Colmenar Viejo are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962382925/presa-de-manzanares-el-real-circular-from-colmenar-via-vias-verdes-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Manzanares el Real – circular from Colmenar via Vías Verdes – gravel&lt;/a&gt; route, which offers a great loop experience.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover on a touring bike ride in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Colmenar Viejo has several historical points of interest. You might encounter archaeological sites, the Basilica de Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, or the Ermita de Remedios. The medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable attraction nearby, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10430811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall – Batán Bridge loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic Batán Bridge.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region's location at the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama means many routes offer stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2497364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cerro de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent hill known for its scenic views, and routes around it provide rewarding panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19828700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Manzanares el Real Viewpoint loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; also offers great viewpoints.

Q: What is the average duration for touring cycling routes in Colmenar Viejo?
A: Route durations vary significantly depending on distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962382925/presa-de-manzanares-el-real-circular-from-colmenar-via-vias-verdes-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Manzanares el Real – circular from Colmenar via Vías Verdes – gravel&lt;/a&gt; route typically takes around 2 hours 33 minutes, while longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10430811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall – Batán Bridge loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Colmenar Viejo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Colmenar Viejo are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural settings within the Manzanares Upper Basin Regional Park, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs with stunning views of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Colmenar Viejo is well-suited for experienced touring cyclists looking for a challenge. Komoot lists 47 difficult routes in the area. These often feature significant elevation changes and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5316936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Hermitage – Hoya de San Blas loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes from the Colmenar Viejo train station?
A: Yes, some routes are conveniently accessible from urban areas, including the train station. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14903199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar Viejo Train Station – Valdelaganar Viewpoint loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that starts directly from the train station, making it easy to begin your adventure upon arrival.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on touring cycling routes in Colmenar Viejo?
A: You'll experience a diverse range of landscapes. Routes often traverse extensive grasslands, areas shaped by traditional granite mining, and offer continuous views of the Sierra de Guadarrama. A significant portion of the area is within the highly protected Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, ensuring pristine natural settings.
